name: Sour-Hot Soup (Suan La Tang)
recipeIngredient: 1 tsp Dry sherry or rice wine
4 tbsp Cornstarch (mixed with 5 tbsp Cold stock or water)
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the mushrooms, cloud ear fungus, and lily flowers by soaking them in hot water for 25 to 30 minutes or until soft. Reserve mushroom liquid as specified.
2. Remove stems from mushrooms; discard. Pick off tough ends of cloud ear; discard ends. Coarse chop cloud ear. Remove tough ends of lily flowers; cut flowers in half. Set aside until ready to cook.
3. Slice pork thin, then cut into fine shreds. Place in a small bowl and mix with wine, soy sauce, sugar, and cornstarch to coat evenly.
4. Slice bean curd in thirds, cut each slice in half, then cut each half into shreds. Mix up the cornstarch paste with cornstarch and stock or water; set aside.
5. Combine stock, mushroom liquid, vinegar, soy sauce, and black pepper. Bring to simmer; add shredded bean curd; mix with chopsticks to keep bean curd from sticking together. Simmer for 2 minutes.
6. Heat a wok and add peanut oil. Cook for 10 seconds, then add shredded pork; stir vigorously until the pork loses its brown color and turns white.
7. Add mushrooms, cloud ear fungus, lily flowers, and bamboo to the wok over high heat for 15 seconds. Stir until combined with the stock.
8. Return the stock to simmer and gradually thicken using the cornstarch mixture, adding a little at a time until the desired thickness is reached. Turn off the heat.
9. Slowly add beaten eggs, folding a little at a time into the soup. Top with diced scallions and sesame oil. Serve immediately.
